sourceMovie.title: WarGames
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto WarGames (1983) as a thriller theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The thriller and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Reloaded
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If WarGames clicked for you, The Matrix Reloaded (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If WarGames clicked for you, eXistenZ (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after WarGames?
faq.answer: Try The Matrix Reloaded, eXistenZ, Outside the Wire—each shares DNA with WarGames while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is WarGames?
faq.answer: WarGames (1983) is best described as Thriller, Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is WarGames part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "WarGames Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
